programs.title: Oceanfront Property Hunt
programs.desc: Katie and Matt thought they had found their dream home, but Katie realized one thing was missing, the ocean; having grown up by the water, she is determined to give her children the same coastal childhood; now they are leaving their perfect inland home behind, ready to trade some luxury for life by the sea; will they find the perfect oceanfront home for their family?

programs.title: Oceanfront Property Hunt
programs.desc: Laura and Trevor met on a tree planting jobsite; they fell in love and built a tree planting business that operates throughout Canada; after deciding to move to Nova Scotia, where Laura is from, they decided to rent because their work kept them traveling throughout most of the year; after many years of saving, they feel like they can finally find a place to put down their own roots and smell the ocean breeze.

programs.title: Oceanfront Property Hunt
programs.desc: College sweethearts Amy and Aron never imagined they would be empty nesters in their 40's, but here they are with two children about to move out and their dreams of retiring by the ocean closer than ever; they have decided to live that dream now and are on the hunt for a home by the water with enough land to have a few goats.

programs.title: Oceanfront Property Hunt
programs.desc: Growing up in England, living by the water was only a dream for the wealthy; after moving to Nova Scotia as a teenager, that dream of ocean living became something that might one day be accessible to her.

programs.title: Oceanfront Property Hunt
programs.desc: Long-time friends and now partners, Kirby and Menna are on the hunt for their perfect home together; they are both avid water enthusiasts and want a home where they can have great parties.

programs.title: Oceanfront Property Hunt
programs.desc: Nova Scotia natives Kim and Ryan have wanted an oceanfront home for as long as they have been together. They decided to downsize their home to buy a boat and live closer to the water. Even after downsizing, they still couldn't afford a home right on the water. Now, after years of saving, they are in the right financial place to look for their dream oceanfront home and a place to park their boat permanently.
